source.title: Pipe Racker Lifting Incident - H&P 406 Rig, Auger TLP

context.region: Gulf of Mexico - Garden Banks Block 426A

event.top_event: Structural damage to drill pipe during pipe racker lifting operations
event.incident_type: Equipment failure due to human error
actions_taken: Damaged drill pipe removed from service and sent for repair
Tripping in the hole operations discontinued
Updated Job By Design Template implemented
event.summary: On 10 December 2021 at 0030 hours, a lifting incident occurred during Well A019 tripping in the hole operations on H&P Rig 406 fixed on Shell's Auger TLP. A single joint of 3.5-inch drill pipe was damaged when the Lower Pipe Racker Operator (LPRO) pulled his end ahead of the Upper Pipe Racker Operator (UPRO), causing the top of the drill pipe stand to get under the elevators as the traveling block was coming down. The pipe bowed and threads were damaged. No injuries occurred. Root causes included LPRO inexperience (second operation, 8 months on rig), inadequate Job Safety Analysis, and inadequate Job By Design Template that failed to specify waiting until block is below fingerboard before removing next stand.
recommendations: Update Job By Design Template to require stand of drill pipe to stay in slot until block is lowered below fingerboard before bringing out next stand
Update Job Safety Analysis to identify potential hazards of impact between elevators and drill pipe when traveling down while simultaneously removing pipe from fingerboard
Include information related to inexperienced employees in JSA

hazards.name: Pressurized hydraulic system with moving mechanical components
Heavy suspended load (drill pipe stand) during tripping operations
Traveling block and elevators in proximity to pipe racking area

threats.name: Uncoordinated movement of pipe racker operators causing pipe misalignment
Premature removal of drill pipe stand from fingerboard while block descending
Inadequate operator experience and training on pipe racker system
Lack of hazard identification in safety procedures for block/pipe interaction

consequences.name: Structural damage to drill pipe (bowing and thread damage)
Loss of equipment availability and operational downtime
Potential personnel injury from falling or struck-by hazard
Financial loss from equipment repair and operational delays

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA)
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to identify hazards and safe work procedures

supporting_text: the JSA failed to identify potential hazards of impact between the elevators with a stand of drill pipe in the when it is traveling down while a stand of drill pipe is simultaneously being removed from the fingerboard
The JSA did not include information related to inexperienced employees

controls.name: Job By Design (JBD) Template
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to specify safe sequence of operations

supporting_text: the JBD Template used before the incident failed to alert that 'When pulling stands from any/all rows wait till block is below finger board to bring stand out of finger row'
an inadequate JBD Template that failed to alert that 'When pulling stands from any/all rows wait till block is below finger board to bring stand out of finger row'

controls.name: Pipe Racking System Manual
controls.barrier_role: Reference documentation for proper equipment operation

supporting_text: The rig keeps the Pipe Racking System Manual on site that provides information for the proper operations of the pipe racker. However, BSEE was told by Shell in an email on 22 April 2022 that the manual is used more for maintenance and not for operations

controls.name: On The Job Training (OJT) Program
controls.barrier_role: Training control to develop operator competence

supporting_text: BSEE confirmed in an email from Shell on 11 May 2022 that the LPRO was trained by a On The Job Training program
This was only the second time that LPRO operated the H&P 406 pipe racker and has been working on the rig for only 8 months performing other non-pipe handling duties

controls.name: Hand-held radio communication between LPRO and UPRO
controls.barrier_role: Communication control to coordinate synchronized pipe movement

supporting_text: Shell informed BSEE that the LPRO and UPRO coordinate their pipe racking operations in their control consoles using a hand-held radios
H&P's Investigation Report stated that a contributing factor was miscommunication between the LPRO and UPRO that caused them to not synchronize the movement of the pipe
BSEE could not substantiate this claim since there was no evidence to support the miscommunication claim

controls.name: Driller supervision and block position monitoring
controls.barrier_role: Operational control to monitor block position and pipe placement

supporting_text: as the Driller was running a stand of drill pipe in the hole while the LPRO and UPRO grabbed the next stand of drill pipe from the finger board closest to the traveling block
the Driller slacked off the previous stand

controls.name: Physical design of pipe racker and block positioning
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to prevent pipe contact with block/elevators

supporting_text: inadequate design of equipment given that the equipment was designed in such a way that flexing of a stand of drill pipe due to momentum while moving in a racker can potentially cause damage to personnel and equipment by impacting the block when traveling down

controls.name: Mechanical retention of pipe by upper and lower racker heads
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to prevent dropped load

supporting_text: The stand of drill pipe did not fall because it was still being held by the upper racker head; therefore, it was retrieved by the lower racker head and was re-racked in the derrick

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
